items.title: The Chicago Sky couldn’t win with Angel Reese.
items.author: Julia Poe
items.body_excerpt:
The Chicago Sky couldn’t win with Angel Reese. 

Now, they can’t win without her.

After a loss to the Atlanta Dream, it’s unclear what was gained — or learned — from trading Reese away.

media.title: As Chicago Sky lose to Atlanta Dream, it’s still unclear what was gained — or learned — in Angel Reese trade
media.description: Chicago fans loved Angel Reese, but they loved her most when she played for the Sky. Now, she’s simply the latest addition to a lengthy list of beloved former stars.

items.title: After a great college career that suggested he had a real chance to be a good NBA player, Jalen Brunson was nonetheless taken way too late in his NBA Draft.
items.author: Matt Norlander
items.body_excerpt:
After a great college career that suggested he had a real chance to be a good NBA player, Jalen Brunson was nonetheless taken way too late in his NBA Draft.

On today's EOCBB, we'll look at the players with similar draft profiles who are destined to be taken too low. Starts at 10 a.m. ET.

Asked about Skylar Diggins’ workload following Tuesday’s loss, Tyler Marsh hinted that Courtney Vandersloot’s return may not be far off.

“We got somebody on the way pretty soon.”

Vandersloot went through full practice last week.

items.title: June 10, 1992: The Blazers take their first lead with 3:34 left in Game 4 of the NBA Finals, and wouldn't trail again in their 93-88 win over the Bulls.
items.author: NBA Cobwebs
items.body_excerpt:
June 10, 1992: The Blazers take their first lead with 3:34 left in Game 4 of the NBA Finals, and wouldn't trail again in their 93-88 win over the Bulls.

Clyde Drexler and Jerome Kersey each scored 21 PTS as Portland tied the series at 2-2. Michael Jordan led Chicago with 32 PTS.

items.title: [Deveney] Increasingly, word is that the Lakers like a lot of the players they have on hand, and rather than making sweeping changes to the roster, could instead bring back a lot of last season's players with just one midrange addition worth in the $20 million AAV range, likely for a big man.
items.author: aingenevalostatrade
items.body_excerpt: Even though the Los Angeles Lakers are projected to have roughly $50 million of salary cap space this summer, it will not be easy for them to improve their roster. Not only will they want to keep LeBron James, but they also have other players about to hit the market they will…

items.title: Hollander POTD Jay Vincent. Michigan St F was drafted 24th in 81 by DAL. Scored 21 ppg as a rook but lost his job to Mark Aguirre & became a bench scorer for DAL thru 86. Bounced around as bench guy thru 90. Played in Italy from 90-94. Teammate of Magic on 79 title team.
items.author: HoopsAnalyst
items.body_excerpt: Hollander POTD Jay Vincent. Michigan St F was drafted 24th in 81 by DAL. Scored 21 ppg as a rook but lost his job to Mark Aguirre & became a bench scorer for DAL thru 86. Bounced around as bench guy thru 90. Played in Italy from 90-94. Teammate of Magic on 79 title team.

items.title: Mike Gansey wore a suit and slicked back hair that would’ve made Pat Riley blush.
items.author: Austin Krell
items.body_excerpt:
Mike Gansey wore a suit and slicked back hair that would’ve made Pat Riley blush.

Bob Myers dressed like someone who answers to billionaires on a day-to-day basis.

Jameer Nelson? A hat and a sweatshirt would suffice.

It’s not a bad thing. It’s symbolic:

www.si.com/nba/76ers/on...

media.title: With a Sweatshirt and Hat, Jameer Nelson Is Ready to Disrupt in the Sixers' Front Office

items.title: GAME 4 TONIGHT 🍿
items.author: NBA
items.body_excerpt:
GAME 4 TONIGHT 🍿

Wemby and Stephon Castle combined for 55 points in San Antonio’s Game 3 win at MSG, the 3rd consecutive road W to open the series.

Will the Knicks take a 3-1 lead or will the Spurs even things up at 2-2?

Don't miss Game 4 of the NBA Finals at 8:30pm/et on ABC 🏆

items.title: In Game 3, Victor Wembanyama recorded his 10th straight game with at least 1 point, 1 rebound, 1 assist, 1 steal and 1 block.
items.author: NBA Stat
items.body_excerpt:
In Game 3, Victor Wembanyama recorded his 10th straight game with at least 1 point, 1 rebound, 1 assist, 1 steal and 1 block.

Only three players have recorded such a streak in a single postseason:
Julius Erving (1980)
LeBron James (2016)
Giannis Antetokounmpo (2019)

(via CrazyStats)
#NBA
